subtopics.title: Binomial Expansion
common_misconceptions: Binomial theorem only works for positive integers
Pascal's triangle is the only way to find coefficients
subtopics.teaching_note: Connect Pascal's triangle to combinations formula. Show applications in approximation and probability.

subtopics.title: Matrix Operations
common_misconceptions: Matrix multiplication is commutative
Any two matrices can be multiplied
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that matrix multiplication is not commutative. Practice with real-world examples like transition matrices.

subtopics.title: Determinants and Inverse Matrices
common_misconceptions: A matrix always has an inverse
Determinant is the same as the matrix
subtopics.teaching_note: Show that only square matrices with non-zero determinants have inverses. Connect to solving simultaneous equations.

subtopics.title: Operations with Complex Numbers
common_misconceptions: i is a variable like x
Complex numbers are not real so they have no use
subtopics.teaching_note: Define i = √-1. Use Argand diagrams to visualize complex numbers. Show applications in engineering and physics.

subtopics.title: Applications of Complex Numbers
common_misconceptions: De Moivre's theorem only works for integer powers
Complex roots always come in pairs
subtopics.teaching_note: Demonstrate De Moivre's theorem for finding powers and roots. Connect to trigonometric identities.

subtopics.title: Principle of Mathematical Induction
common_misconceptions: Checking a few examples is sufficient proof
The base case is always n=1
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize both steps are necessary. Use domino analogy. Practice with summation and divisibility proofs.

subtopics.title: Parabola and Ellipse
common_misconceptions: Parabola only opens upward
Ellipse and circle are completely different
subtopics.teaching_note: Show conic sections as slices of a cone. Derive standard equations from geometric definitions.

subtopics.title: Hyperbola
common_misconceptions: Hyperbola is two separate curves
Asymptotes are lines the curve never approaches
subtopics.teaching_note: Explain asymptotes clearly. Connect to real-world applications like navigation (LORAN) and astronomy.

subtopics.title: Limits and Continuity
common_misconceptions: Limit is the same as function value
A function cannot have a limit at a discontinuity
subtopics.teaching_note: Use graphical and numerical approaches to understand limits. Distinguish between limit and function value.

subtopics.title: Differentiation Rules and Applications
common_misconceptions: Derivative is the same as the gradient of a line
The product rule is d(uv)/dx = du/dx × dv/dx
subtopics.teaching_note: Practice each rule with varied examples. Connect derivatives to real-world rates of change. Use second derivative for curve sketching.

subtopics.title: Indefinite Integration
common_misconceptions: Integration is just the reverse of differentiation without the constant
Integration by parts always works
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize the constant of integration. Teach the LIATE rule for choosing u and dv in integration by parts.

subtopics.title: Definite Integration and Applications
common_misconceptions: Definite integral always gives area
Area below x-axis is positive
subtopics.teaching_note: Show that definite integrals can be negative. Emphasize taking absolute value for area calculations.
